name: sdlc-plugin
description: Software Development Lifecycle skills, bundled. Ten skills covering idea-to-backlog, provisioning, implementation, audit, and course-correction: /repo-bootstrap (new GitHub repo from scratch), /repo-level-up (add release/uat + release/prod branches, promotion workflows, secret scanning, branch-protection rulesets), /initial-design (first-stab architectural design into docs/architecture/ — system type, components, hosting, stack, data, integrations, lightweight ADRs, open questions; bridges the gap between README and requirements so the technical shape isn't assumed in a vacuum), /provision-platform (stands up the cloud/SaaS platforms the architecture names — hosting, observability, DBs, auth, email, etc.; tries every available channel including connected MCP servers, CLIs, and HTTP APIs; batches human-only steps like account creation and secret pasting into one checklist; wires resulting secrets into GitHub Actions and platform env stores; records everything in docs/architecture/provisioning-log.md), /create-requirements (interactively elicit functional + non-functional requirements into docs/requirements/ — defaults to README.md as the source, asks for an alternative if missing; reads docs/architecture/ if present so requirements respect the recorded architecture), /confirm-requirements (validate and advance the status of existing requirements; cross-checks against docs/architecture/ for drift), /tasks-from-requirements (turn the requirements into a staged plan of small-batch GitHub issues with milestones, labels, and human-required tasks front-loaded), /implement-task (heavyweight end-to-end implementation of one GitHub issue via a multi-persona orchestration — Principal Engineer, QA Engineer, Cloud Architect, UX/UI Designer, Test Automation Engineer, Project Manager, and Work Checker — all reading docs/architecture/ before their phases; audit-trail comments, diligent PM bounce-back gate, Playwright-based UX verification), /check-work (generic 'please just check your work carefully' second-pass skill that re-examines whatever was just produced — code, plans, writing, analysis, anything — and reports issues specifically; saves the user typing the phrase), /rework (when an implementation-time discovery invalidates the plan — assertively cleans up stale requirements and issues, with a documented audit trail).
